Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents a single product within the Sophia E-Hub, storing both imported raw data and calculated analytical metrics.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

sku.description: Stock Keeping Unit, a unique identifier for a product in an e-commerce context.

productName.description: The commercial name of the product.

marketplace.description: The marketplace where the product is sold (e.g., 'Mercado Livre', 'Amazon').

brand.description: The brand name of the product.

shippingType.description: The type of shipping offered for the product (e.g., 'Standard', 'Express').

salePrice.description: The price at which the product is sold.

productCost.description: The cost of acquiring or producing the product.

marketplaceCommission.description: The commission percentage or fixed fee charged by the marketplace.

logisticCost.description: The cost associated with logistics (storage, packaging, shipping).

adsInvestment.description: The investment made in advertising for this specific product.

complaintPercentage.description: The percentage of sales that resulted in a customer complaint for this product.

marginPercentage.description: The calculated profit margin as a percentage of the sale price.

netProfit.description: The calculated net profit after all direct costs and commissions.

roas.description: The calculated Return on Ad Spend for the product. (Calculated based on 'salePrice' and 'adsInvestment').

score.description: A strategic score assigned to the product based on its margin, influencing its status.

status.description: The operational status of the product (APROVADO, ATENÇÃO, CRÍTICO) based on its score.

abcClassification.description: The classification of the product according to the ABC curve analysis (A, B, C).

complaintStatus.description: The status related to customer complaints (OK, OBSERVAR, ALERTA) based on 'complaintPercentage'.

lastUpdatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the product data was last imported or recalculated.

CompanyConfiguration.title: CompanyConfiguration
CompanyConfiguration.description: Stores global settings and preferences for the Sophia E-Hub application, specific to the company using it.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CompanyConfiguration entity.

companyName.description: The official name of the company using the Sophia E-Hub.

corporateName.description: The full corporate name or legal entity name of the company.

cnpj.description: The Brazilian National Registry of Legal Entities (CNPJ) number for the company.

logoUrl.description: The URL of the company's logo, used for branding within the application.

themePreference.description: User's preferred theme for the application interface (e.g., 'dark', 'light').

systemPrimaryColor.description: The primary color used for branding and interactive elements in the system's UI (e.g., hex code).

appVersion.description: The current version of the Sophia E-Hub application.

lastUpdateDate.description: The date of the last application update or change.

dashboardDefaultPeriod.description: The default time period for displaying data on the dashboard (e.g., 'daily', 'weekly', 'monthly', 'custom').

dashboardFavoriteMetrics.description: A list of preferred metrics to display by default on the dashboard.

reportDefaultFormat.description: The default file format for exporting reports (e.g., 'CSV', 'XLSX', 'PDF').

IntegrationSetting.title: IntegrationSetting
IntegrationSetting.description: Stores API credentials and connection status for various marketplace integrations.

id.description: Unique identifier for the IntegrationSetting entity.

marketplaceName.description: The name of the marketplace for which the integration is configured (e.g., 'Mercado Livre', 'Amazon').

clientId.description: The client ID for API authentication with the marketplace.

clientSecret.description: The client secret for API authentication with the marketplace. (Sensitive data).

accessToken.description: The access token for making API calls to the marketplace. (Sensitive data).

refreshToken.description: The refresh token used to obtain new access tokens when the current one expires. (Sensitive data).

connectionStatus.description: The current status of the connection to the marketplace API (e.g., 'Connected', 'Disconnected', 'Error').

lastConnectionAttempt.description: Timestamp of the last attempt to connect or refresh tokens with the marketplace API.

ReportTemplate.title: ReportTemplate
ReportTemplate.description: Allows users to save and reuse configurations for frequently generated reports.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ReportTemplate entity.

templateName.description: A user-defined name for the report template (e.g., 'Daily Sales Report', 'Critical Products Report').

reportType.description: The frequency or period type for the report (e.g., 'daily', 'weekly', 'monthly', 'period').

scopeType.description: Defines which products are included in the report (e.g., 'all_products', 'selected_products', 'critical_products', 'approved_products', 'abc_a', 'abc_b', 'abc_c').

selectedProductIds.description: An array of Product IDs if the 'scopeType' is 'selected_products'. (Relationship: ReportTemplate N:N Product)

selectedMetrics.description: An array of metric names (e.g., 'revenue', 'netProfit', 'margin', 'roas', 'score', 'status', 'complaints', 'abcClassification', 'alerts') to be included in the report.

startDate.description: The start date for a custom period report, if applicable.

endDate.description: The end date for a custom period report, if applicable.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the report template was created.

lastModifiedAt.description: Timestamp when the report template was last modified.

DataImport.title: DataImport
DataImport.description: Records metadata about each data import operation performed in the Sophia E-Hub.

id.description: Unique identifier for the DataImport entity.

importDate.description: The date and time when the data import was performed.

fileName.description: The name of the file that was imported.

fileType.description: The type of the imported file (e.g., 'CSV', 'XLSX', 'Google Sheets').

status.description: The outcome status of the import operation (e.g., 'Success', 'Failed', 'Processing').

errorMessage.description: A message detailing any error that occurred during the import, if the status is 'Failed'.

numRecordsImported.description: The number of new product records created during this import.

numRecordsUpdated.description: The number of existing product records updated during this import.

numRecordsFailed.description: The number of records that failed to import or update due to validation or processing errors.

definition.description: Stores global settings and preferences for the Sophia E-Hub application, specific to the user's company. Owned by the user, facilitating direct authorization via the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this company's configuration.

params.name: companyConfigurationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the company configuration document.

definition.description: Represents a single product, storing both imported raw data and calculated analytical metrics. Data is specific to the user's company and owned by the user, enabling simple path-based authorization.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this product data.

params.name: productId
params.description: The unique identifier for the product document.

definition.description: Stores API credentials and connection status for various marketplace integrations. Each setting is specific to the user's company and secured by path-based ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this integration setting.

params.name: integrationSettingId
params.description: The unique identifier for the integration setting document.

definition.description: Allows users to save and reuse configurations for frequently generated reports. Templates are specific to the user's company and secured via path-based ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this report template.

params.name: reportTemplateId
params.description: The unique identifier for the report template document.

definition.description: Records metadata about each data import operation performed in the Sophia E-Hub. Import history is specific to the user's company and accessible only by the owning user.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who performed this data import.

params.name: dataImportId
params.description: The unique identifier for the data import record.

firestore.reasoning:
The Sophia E-Hub Firestore structure is designed with a strong emphasis on authorization independence, debuggability, and QAP compliance, primarily achieved through path-based ownership. All primary data entities (CompanyConfiguration, Product, IntegrationSetting, ReportTemplate, DataImport) are stored as subcollections under a user-specific path: `/users/{userId}/<collectionName>/{documentId}`. This structure directly ties every document to its owner (the authenticated user), adhering to the 'Private Data' access model.

Authorization Independence is achieved because the `{userId}` segment in the path serves as the denormalized authorization context for all documents within that user's subcollections. Firestore Security Rules can directly compare `request.auth.uid` with the `{userId}` wildcard in the path, eliminating the need for `get()` calls to check parent document attributes for authorization. This makes rules atomic, highly performant, and significantly easier to write and debug.

Queryable Access Patterns (QAPs) are robustly supported by this structural segregation. Since all data is organized under `/users/{userId}/...`, any `list` operation initiated by an authenticated user (`request.auth.uid`) targeting their specific subcollections (e.g., `/users/{request.auth.uid}/products`) will inherently only retrieve data belonging to that user. The security rules, by enforcing `allow list: if request.auth.uid == userId;`, ensure that clients cannot accidentally or maliciously query data from other users' paths. This guarantees that `list` operations are secure by design, providing only data that the authenticated user is authorized to access, without relying on client-side filtering or complex server-side pre-processing after data retrieval.
